tests.expectedFailure: Under tsx with a STATIC `import * as m from "@repo/module-management-workflow"`, the barrel's `export * from "./design"` (no extension) fails to resolve and yields undefined for every design symbol. Observed: loadSession=undefined, executeCreateModuleWorkflow=function. Test asserts loadSession=function and executeCreateModuleWorkflow=function via a tsx subprocess that runs from apps/cli (where the workspace symlink resolves).

apps/cli/src/index.ts has a top-level eager import `import { runModuleConsistency, runModuleDesign } from "./commands/module-design/index.js"` (line 18). Tests assert (a) no top-level `import { ... runModuleConsistency ... } from ...`, (b) no top-level `import { ... runModuleDesign ... } from ...`, (c) the literal string `from "./commands/module-design/index.js"` does not appear in non-comment code, (d) `await import(...)` for runModuleDesign is present inside the module create .action() callback, (e) `await import(...)` for runModuleConsistency is present inside the module consistency .action() callback.

apps/cli/src/commands/module-design/consistency.ts declares an inline `function loadSession(moduleDir: string): DesignSessionState | null { ... }` shim (lines 14-25) and does NOT import loadSession from @repo/module-management-workflow. Tests assert (a) no `function loadSession(` declaration, (b) the source contains `import { ..., loadSession, ... } from "@repo/module-management-workflow"`.

edge-cases: extensionless barrel re-export under strict ESM (tsx static import)
subprocess-based resolution check (vitest in-process dynamic import is lenient and would not catch the bug)
security: lazy-load invariant: a broken subcommand barrel (e.g. throw on import) must not kill baka list-modules / --help / etc.
integration: CLI top-level source (apps/cli/src/index.ts) does not eagerly pull in the module-design subgraph
consistency.ts uses the single-source-of-truth loadSession from @repo/module-management-workflow
